table.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top;

tr.style: border: 1px #F0F0F0 solid; font-size: 11px; font-family: verdana; vertical-align: top

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: The logical name of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Information about the use of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

a.title: Minimum and Maximum # of times the element can appear in the instance

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px; width: 100px

a.title: Reference to the type of the element

th.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; padding-top: 3px; padding-bottom: 3px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck1.png)
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck134.png)

span.title: This element must be supported

tr.td:
Island where the address is located.
URL: https://healthinfoservices.net/pacific-base-ig/StructureDefinition/pacific-address-island

Geographic coordinates of the address.

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: The complete address as written or spoken locally. In many Pacific contexts this is the primary form of the address and may include village, island, landmarks, or other narrative description.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

span.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; color: white; background-color: red

tr.td: Full descriptive address, should be considered the primary representation of the address

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: white

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: Address line components used for landmarks, household names, compound identifiers, street names, or other free-text address components.

tr.td: Free text address lines may refer to proximity to landmarks (e.g.school, church etc.)

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

a.title: The city or town for the address. In rural areas this element may be absent.

a.title: Administrative subdivision below the province or state level. May represent district, ward, or similar administrative unit depending on national administrative structures.

span.title: This element must be supported

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

tr.td: Province, division, or first-level administrative region.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: white; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck10.png)

tr.td: Postal code is often not used, so is currently optional and may be removed if not required in the future

tr.style: border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px; vertical-align: top; background-color: #F7F7F7

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px; white-space: nowrap; background-image: url(tbl_bck00.png)

a.title: Country expressed using ISO 3166 two-letter country codes.

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

span.style: padding-left: 3px; padding-right: 3px; color: white; background-color: red
span.title: This element must be supported

td.style: vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px
vertical-align: top; text-align : var(--ig-left,left); background-color: #F7F7F7; border: 0px #F0F0F0 solid; padding:0px 4px 0px 4px

tr.td: Country is required for international interoperability. Use ISO 3166 two-letter country codes.

description.value:
Address profile designed for Pacific nations where addresses are commonly descriptive and village-based rather than street-based. This profile supports a range of address formats commonly used in Pacific jurisdictions, with commonly required extensions, and general usage guidance. Each national core IG derived from this base profile should further specify mappings of administrative hierarchies to the specific address fields In all cases: - text is a mandatory field and should be considered the primary representation of the fully address. - line is used for free text address lines and may relate to a landmark, school, church etc. rather than a street address. - Village and Island are added as extensions to the base fhir Address definition to support common Pacific address formats. - clanAffiliation is already an extension in the PacificPatient, PacificProvider profiles so does not need to be duplicated here. - geolocation is added as an optional extension to support use case where GPS coordinates are available. It is expected that this will become more common. - Postal code is often not used, so is currently optional and may be removed (ie 0..0) if not required in the future - Country is required to support international interoperability
Recommended mapping of address fields are below but should be specifically defined and documented in national core IGs
| FHIR Element | Typical Pacific Use | Notes | | ------------------- | ------------------------------ | ------------------------------ | | `text` | Full narrative address | Often the primary address form | | `line` | Landmark, compound, street | Free-text | | `city` | Town | Mainly urban areas | | `district` | Ward / district / area council | Country dependent | | `state` | Province / division | Country dependent | | `extension:village` | Village | Most rural addresses | | `extension:island` | Island | Important for logistics |

short.value: An address expressed using postal conventions (as opposed to GPS or other location definition formats)

definition.value: An address expressed using postal conventions (as opposed to GPS or other location definition formats). This data type may be used to convey addresses for use in delivering mail as well as for visiting locations which might not be valid for mail delivery. There are a variety of postal address formats defined around the world.

comment.value: Note: address is intended to describe postal addresses for administrative purposes, not to describe absolute geographical coordinates. Postal addresses are often used as proxies for physical locations (also see the [Location](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4B/location.html#) resource).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children unless an empty Parameters resource

expression.value: hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()) or $this is Parameters

short.value: Unique id for inter-element referencing

definition.value: Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

description.value: Extensions are always sliced by (at least) url

short.value: Additional content defined by implementations

definition.value: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

comment.value: There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Village or rural settlement.

definition.value: Village, settlement, or rural community name. This is often a primary geographic identifier in Pacific addressing.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Island where the address is located.

definition.value: Island name where the address is located. This is important in archipelago nations where logistics and travel depend on island geography.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: Geographic coordinates of the address.

definition.value: Latitude and longitude coordinates representing the geographic location of the address. May represent household, facility, or village centroid.

comment.value: The extension can be further extended to include unique geolocation identifiers, confidence, altitude, etc.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children
Must have either extensions or value[x], not both

short.value: home | work | temp | old | billing - purpose of this address

definition.value: The purpose of this address.

comment.value: Applications can assume that an address is current unless it explicitly says that it is temporary or old.

requirements.value: Allows an appropriate address to be chosen from a list of many.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

isModifierReason.value: This is labeled as "Is Modifier" because applications should not mistake a temporary or old address etc.for a current/permanent one

description.value: The use of an address (home / work / etc.).

short.value: postal | physical | both

definition.value: Distinguishes between physical addresses (those you can visit) and mailing addresses (e.g. PO Boxes and care-of addresses). Most addresses are both.

comment.value: The definition of Address states that "address is intended to describe postal addresses, not physical locations". However, many applications track whether an address has a dual purpose of being a location that can be visited as well as being a valid delivery destination, and Postal addresses are often used as proxies for physical locations (also see the [Location](http://hl7.org/fhir/R4B/location.html#) resource).

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

valueString.value: The type of an address (physical / postal).

description.value: The type of an address (physical / postal).

short.value: Full descriptive address, should be considered the primary representation of the address

definition.value: The complete address as written or spoken locally. In many Pacific contexts this is the primary form of the address and may include village, island, landmarks, or other narrative description.

comment.value: Can provide both a text representation and parts. Applications updating an address SHALL ensure that when both text and parts are present, no content is included in the text that isn't found in a part.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: XAD.1 + XAD.2 + XAD.3 + XAD.4 + XAD.5 + XAD.6

short.value: Free text address lines may refer to proximity to landmarks (e.g.school, church etc.)

definition.value: Address line components used for landmarks, household names, compound identifiers, street names, or other free-text address components.

orderMeaning.value: The order in which lines should appear in an address label

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

map.value: XAD.1 + XAD.2 (note: XAD.1 and XAD.2 have different meanings for a company address than for a person address)

short.value: Town or urban locality.

definition.value: The city or town for the address. In rural areas this element may be absent.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: District, ward, or sub-provincial administrative area.

definition.value: Administrative subdivision below the province or state level. May represent district, ward, or similar administrative unit depending on national administrative structures.

comment.value: District is sometimes known as county, but in some regions 'county' is used in place of city (municipality), so county name should be conveyed in city instead.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Province, division, or first-level administrative region.

definition.value: First-level administrative region such as province, division, or region depending on national geography.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Postal code is often not used, so is currently optional and may be removed if not required in the future

definition.value: A postal code designating a region defined by the postal service.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Country is required for international interoperability. Use ISO 3166 two-letter country codes.

definition.value: Country expressed using ISO 3166 two-letter country codes.

comment.value: ISO 3166 3 letter codes can be used in place of a human readable country name.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Time period when address was/is in use

definition.value: Time period when address was/is in use.

requirements.value: Allows addresses to be placed in historical context.

human.value: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children

short.value: Village or rural settlement.

definition.value: Village, settlement, or rural community name. This is often a primary geographic identifier in Pacific addressing.

short.value: Island where the address is located.

definition.value: Island name where the address is located. This is important in archipelago nations where logistics and travel depend on island geography.

short.value: Geographic coordinates of the address.

definition.value: Latitude and longitude coordinates representing the geographic location of the address. May represent household, facility, or village centroid.

short.value: Full descriptive address, should be considered the primary representation of the address

definition.value: The complete address as written or spoken locally. In many Pacific contexts this is the primary form of the address and may include village, island, landmarks, or other narrative description.

short.value: Free text address lines may refer to proximity to landmarks (e.g.school, church etc.)

definition.value: Address line components used for landmarks, household names, compound identifiers, street names, or other free-text address components.

short.value: Town or urban locality.

definition.value: The city or town for the address. In rural areas this element may be absent.

short.value: District, ward, or sub-provincial administrative area.

definition.value: Administrative subdivision below the province or state level. May represent district, ward, or similar administrative unit depending on national administrative structures.

short.value: Province, division, or first-level administrative region.

definition.value: First-level administrative region such as province, division, or region depending on national geography.

short.value: Postal code is often not used, so is currently optional and may be removed if not required in the future
Country is required for international interoperability. Use ISO 3166 two-letter country codes.

definition.value: Country expressed using ISO 3166 two-letter country codes.
